literal: the moment or place where a plan or idea is put into practical action and tested in real conditions
a person who is very distantly related or marginally connected by kinship
a willingness to give all one has to help another, even to the point of personal deprivation
a situation in which roles or positions have been reversed between two parties

relabelled_senses.definition: Originally, a principle in theatre meaning that a performance must continue despite difficulties, setbacks, or the absence of performers.

literal: no literal meaning
a final, seemingly minor burden or annoyance that causes a collapse or decisive reaction
a place described as offering abundant wealth or easy prosperity
the final small action or event that causes a large failure or breakdown after many burdens have accumulated
a warning that yielding to terrorists means they have achieved their goal
a discourse marker used to introduce the main point or important consideration
a discourse marker introducing the main point or essential fact
an exaggerated way of saying a great many people or virtually everyone
an announcement that approval or permission to proceed has been given
throughout the world; in all parts of the world
in all circumstances, including good and bad times; regardless of difficulties
very close or friendly; showing strong mutual affection or familiarity
in the middle of activity or events; actively involved in the central part of a situation
a joke or remark that is extremely funny and likely to provoke loud laughter
a small initial action likely to lead to larger undesirable consequences
something that no longer exists or is no longer practiced; obsolete
